svg.text: ANCIENT EGYPTIAN
HISTORY — 4000 YEARS OF CIVILIZATION
ANCIENT EGYPTIAN LANGUAGE TIMELINE
~3200 BCE: earliest hieroglyphic inscriptions (Narmer Palette)
~2600-700 BCE: Old, Middle, Late Egyptian (living language)
~700 BCE-400 CE: Demotic (cursive administrative script)
~200-1300 CE: Coptic (last stage; Greek-alphabet Egyptian)
~1300 CE: Arabic replaces Coptic as vernacular; language "dies"
WORLD'S LONGEST RECORDED LANGUAGE HISTORY
Egyptian: continuous written record ~3200 BCE to ~1300 CE = 4500 years
Longest attested language in human history
Surpasses even Chinese, Sanskrit, and Greek in written continuity
GREAT TEXTS IN EGYPTIAN
Pyramid Texts (~2400 BCE): oldest religious corpus
Book of the Dead (~1550 BCE): guide for the afterlife
Amarna Letters (~1350 BCE): diplomatic correspondence in Akkadian
GREEK HISTORY · SECONDARY LANGUAGE
GREEK IN EGYPT: 1000 YEARS
332 BCE: Alexander founds Alexandria; Greek becomes Egypt's official language
Ptolemaic dynasty: Greek pharaohs for 275 years; bilingual courts
30 BCE-642 CE: Roman then Byzantine rule; Greek remains administrative
Total: ~1000 years of Greek as Egypt's official language
THE SEPTUAGINT AND CHRISTIAN EGYPT
~250 BCE: 70 Jewish scholars translate Torah into Greek (Septuagint)
Translated in Alexandria; became Christian Old Testament
Christianity reached Egypt ~42 CE (tradition: St. Mark in Alexandria)
Coptic Christians: Greek Christian theology in Egyptian language
DECIPHERMENT AND MODERN LEGACY
1799: Napoleon's soldiers find Rosetta Stone near Rashid
1822: Champollion deciphers using Greek text as key
Without Greek: 4000 years of Egyptian history would still be silent
Greek was the key that opened the oldest written language on Earth
PLATE 61d ALT · ANCIENT EGYPTIAN / GREEK · HISTORY
